input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension on any property:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

Each `AppThemeBinding` takes a `Light` and `Dark` value. MAUI automatically picks the right one based on the OS theme and switches live if the user changes it.

---

**C# alternative** — use `AppThemeBinding` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// Option 1: SetAppThemeColor (colors only)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
myPage.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"), Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

// Option 2: SetBinding with AppThemeBinding (any property type)
myImage.SetBinding(Image.SourceProperty, new AppThemeBindingExtension
{
    Light = "sun.png",
    Dark = "moon.png"
});
```

---

**Key points:**
- No manual event wiring — MAUI monitors `Application.RequestedTheme` automatically.
- Works on all platforms (iOS, Android, Windows, Mac Catalyst).
- For app-wide consistency, define theme colors as resources in `App.xaml` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` instead of repeating hex values everywhere.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about .NET MAUI's `AppThemeBinding` for automatic light/dark theme switching. This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code modification task. I'll provide a concise, practical answer with XAML and C# examples.
